{"society_power": 9.5, "state_power": 9.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Mass democracy completed alongside failed General Strike", "reasoning": "Universal suffrage (1918, 1928) and Labour's rise deepened society's electoral leverage, offsetting union defeats in the 1926 General Strike and 1927 Act, keeping society very strong. State capacity remained high with an expanding welfare bureaucracy, but demobilization from wartime peak and loss of direct Irish territorial control eased state power modestly from its 1918 maximum."}
